title: Fahey
description: Fahey — AI as Administrative Tool (workshop draft)
show_notes:
Fahey — AI as Administrative Tool (workshop draft)

This early-stage workshop draft argues that agencies adopting AI tools are not operating in a legal vacuum: existing statutory delegations and constitutional authorities already supply ample authorization for the government to build and deploy AI in its administrative work, from congressional grants like the Stafford and Post-Katrina emergency-management statutes to the President's executive authority. Fahey's worry is the flip side of that abundance—an 'authorization anachronism' problem in which old statutes, written for an earlier technological era, are stretched to license powerful new AI-powered capabilities, eroding the democratic assumptions and built-in technological limits that once constrained what agencies could do. The draft sketches how AI alters the relationship between substantive authority and the practical capacities that shape what the administrative state can actually accomplish.

Chapters:
0:03 Introduction
7:09 I. A Basic Sketch of AI’s Authorization
19:57 II. Implications
32:11 Conclusion
